etymology.description: 衣("clothes") + 又("hand") = to put on clothes
etymology.interpretation: The character 袁 originally depicted the action of putting on or wearing clothes, with 衣(衣) (yī, "clothes") representing garments and 又(又) (yòu, "hand") indicating the action of using hands to dress. The hand component, which later evolved into other forms, indicates the interaction with clothes, hence forming the meaning of wearing. Over time, this character came to be associated with a long garment and used as a surname.

examples.chinese: 袁先生是一个伟大的作家(袁先生是一個偉大的作家)。
examples.pinyin: Yuán xiānshēng shì yī gè wěidà de zuòjiā.
examples.english: Mr. Yuan is a great writer.

examples.chinese: 他们都称呼他为袁博士(他們都稱呼他為袁博士)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen dōu chēnghū tā wéi Yuán bóshì.
examples.english: They all call him Dr. Yuan.

examples.chinese: 袁家大院有一个美丽的花园(袁家大院有一個美麗的花園)。
examples.pinyin: Yuán jiā dàyuàn yǒu yī gè měilì de huāyuán.
examples.english: The Yuan family's large courtyard has a beautiful garden.
